danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy creatures positioned in environment; hostile NPC sprites with distinct appearance from allies
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to party; avoidable through dialogue or stealth in many cases

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental visual cues indicating planes like Baator and Carceri; corrupted or alien landscape features
danger_signals.meaning: Exposure to dangerous extraplanar forces; narrative danger tied to metaphysical threat

danger_signals.visual_form: The Nameless One sprite transitions through death state; respawn location change signals narrative consequence
danger_signals.meaning: Death is survivable but creates world-state consequence; memory loss persists as narrative danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Civilian characters in Sigil environment; friendly party members accompanying protagonist
safety_signals.meaning: Social safety; dialogue available as problem-solving tool

safety_signals.visual_form: Faction-controlled locations with distinct visual architecture; faction member NPCs present
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety and alliance; access to faction resources and quests

safety_signals.visual_form: Well-rendered painted scenery of Sigil's city districts; contrast with darker planar regions
safety_signals.meaning: Established order; reduced supernatural danger; social interaction possible

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Faction-specific visual markers and robes/insignia on member NPCs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Faction members become hostile if player alignment or reputation conflicts; visual appearance unchanged but behavior shifts
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Faction-controlled districts and headquarters in Sigil; each faction's base reflects ideological identity (e.g., law vs. chaos visual language)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Lady of Pain as visual entity; represents hierarchical control and mystery
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Not directly encountered as hostile; represents omnipotent threat-presence in world-state
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sigil's central layout and spire design reflects her dominion; all-seeing perspective reinforced through environmental design

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Creature design and planar visual language; corrupted or ordered aesthetics based on plane type
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Planar creatures appear in their respective planes (Baator, Carceri); visual manifestation tied to metaphysical alignment
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Baator rendered with hellish aesthetics; Carceri with prison/confinement visuals; visual language indicates moral/metaphysical nature

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Protagonist sprite remains constant despite death; visual indicator that standard mortality rules do not apply
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs reference immortality; story dialogue changes based on The Nameless One's accumulated incarnation knowledge; character alignment shifts visually influence NPC reactions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: NPC character portraits and dialogue text reflect moral stance; visual presentation of alignment through companion reactions and environmental NPC behavior
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs become hostile or friendly based on alignment changes; quest resolution paths alter; party member compatibility shifts visually through conversation animations and stance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition between Sigil (painted, stable aesthetics) and outer planes (visually corrupted or alien); environmental color palette and architecture shift to indicate planar nature
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy encounter frequency increases in dangerous planes; NPC dialogue references planar hazards; party member comments on environmental danger through dialogue triggers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Faction member NPCs display loyalty or hostility through sprite positioning and encounter type; faction headquarters become inaccessible if reputation damaged
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Faction members assist or oppose based on reputation; quests available/unavailable based on alliance status; combat encounters change composition based on faction allegiance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Journal tracks active quests visually; NPCs reference completed actions; environmental state changes (e.g., defeated enemies remain absent from locations)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPC dialogue reflects completed quests; new quest-givers appear; previously hostile NPCs become neutral; party member commentary evolves with story progress

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cutscene and dialogue sequences reveal past incarnations through narrative visuals; environmental storytelling expands with each discovered memory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: The Nameless One gains new dialogue options; companion reactions change based on revealed past; NPC perception of protagonist shifts as history unfolds

environmental_storytelling.example: Sigil's location atop an infinitely tall spire at multiverse center communicated through isometric perspective and portal-based navigation; visual language immediately establishes metaphysical setting without explicit exposition
Each faction's headquarters and territory visually reflects its world-view; architectural style, NPC dress, and environmental details communicate faction philosophy before dialogue occurs
Game opens in The Mortuary; visual design of resurrection/death facility immediately communicates themes of death, immortality, and mystery central to plot

environmental_storytelling.technique: NPC visual design as character background
environmental_storytelling.example: Character sprites and portraits encode background information; party member appearance communicates race, class, and planar origin; visual distinctiveness enables rapid player recognition and emotional attachment
Baator rendered with hellish visual language (red tones, infernal architecture); Carceri with prison/restraint visuals; visual language communicates moral/metaphysical nature without explicit text

environmental_storytelling.technique: Quest journal as diegetic memory system
environmental_storytelling.example: The Nameless One's journal serves both mechanical and narrative function; visual presentation of notes and objectives reinforces amnesia theme and character's struggle to maintain continuity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Dialogue options available across moral spectrum; NPC reactions neutral; wide quest path accessibility
Dialogue options narrow based on alignment; certain NPCs become permanently hostile; quest paths close; companion compatibility changes; visual presentation through NPC reaction animations
Frequent positive inter-party dialogue; companions defend The Nameless One in combat; quest-specific companion dialogue triggers; visual presentation through animated conversations
Companions make critical commentary; reduced combat synergy; potential companion departure; visual presentation through tense dialogue animations and stance changes
Faction members assist in quests; new quests become available; safe passage through faction territory; visual indicator through friendly NPC positioning and dialogue tone
Faction members become combatants; quests become unavailable; faction headquarters become inaccessible; visual indicator through hostile NPC positioning and combat readiness
Creatures display maximum behavioral complexity; full combat encounter or dialogue options available; visual presentation enhanced by environmental immersion

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Encountered outside native plane or summoned
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Creatures may appear confused or weakened; behavioral options limited; visual presentation may include planar distortion effects

notes: Planescape: Torment employs visual communication heavily through environment design, faction aesthetics, and NPC character presentation rather than explicit HUD indicators. The Infinity Engine's isometric pre-rendered aesthetic enables rich environmental storytelling. Alignment system creates core feedback loop: player actions → visual/behavioral NPC response → world-state perception. The immortality mechanic eliminates traditional death penalty, shifting danger communication toward narrative consequence rather than mechanical loss. Planar setting enables dramatic visual contrast between civilized Sigil and hostile extraplanar locations. Faction system uses environmental markers and NPC visual identity to communicate political landscape without explicit faction maps. Journal system serves dual narrative/mechanical function, reinforcing amnesia theme visually.
